summary: Spec and plan for adding is_terminal_state(state) to lib.py: a pure predicate returning True for done/failed/blocked, False for any other input including None and non-strings without raising. One additive function insertion after PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS; one new focused test file covering all 7 acceptance criteria. No call-site refactoring.

ledger.what: `is_terminal_state` does not already exist in any engine file
ledger.why: Would create a duplicate or name collision if it already existed somewhere in the engine
ledger.what_i_did: Grepped the entire repo for `is_terminal_state` — zero matches confirmed; function is net-new

blast_radius.why: Confirms the change is purely additive; no existing symbol is shadowed or overloaded

reversibility.why: A purely additive new function can be deleted in a single-line revert with no downstream impact

ledger.revisit_if: A future branch adds the function under a different name before this merges, creating a collision

ledger.why: Natural grouping: PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS already enumerates the exact three terminal keys; placing the helper directly after creates a self-documenting cluster
ledger.what_i_did: Verified lines 583-593 of lib.py — PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS defines exactly 'done', 'failed', 'blocked' as keys; confirmed the blank line at 593 provides a clean insertion slot before _humanize_state_id at 595

blast_radius.why: Placement is a style decision with zero runtime effect; no callers are affected regardless of where in the module the function appears

reversibility.why: Moving a module-level function to a different position is a trivial one-step refactor at any future point

ledger.revisit_if: Code review suggests a different location (e.g., near advance.py's state-transition logic or at module top)

ledger.why: Handles None and non-string input without exception (isinstance guard short-circuits), O(1) set membership, no imports, pure function
ledger.what_i_did: Verified that a bare `state in {"done","failed","blocked"}` raises no exception for None or int in Python 3 (the `in` operator on a set never raises for hashable inputs); however the isinstance guard is still the clearest expression of the contract and avoids any future surprise if unhashable types are passed

blast_radius.why: Internal implementation detail of a single-line function; no caller is affected by the choice of isinstance vs try/except

reversibility.why: The body is one line; rewriting it is a trivial edit

ledger.revisit_if: Performance profiling shows this is a hot path (not plausible for a string membership check, but listed for completeness)

ledger.why: `test_engine.py` covers CAS/path-CLI concerns; focused per-topic test files are the repo's established pattern (test_decide.py, test_phase_labels.py, test_correlation.py, etc.)
ledger.what_i_did: Reviewed the 55+ test module list in target/tests/; every file has a single-topic name; test_engine.py's module docstring explicitly says it covers 'GENERIC, protocol-agnostic lib surface' for CAS and path-CLI only

blast_radius.why: New file only; no existing tests are modified or deleted; the suite gains 7 tests with no risk of breaking existing ones

reversibility.why: The file can be deleted or merged into test_engine.py without consequence; all tests are self-contained

ledger.revisit_if: Reviewer prefers all lib.py helper tests consolidated into test_engine.py

ledger.what: `"blocked"` is a proper terminal state, not only a label alias
ledger.why: The issue lists 'blocked' as one of the three terminal states; must verify it is treated as an end-state in the engine, not merely a display key
ledger.what_i_did: Verified: (1) PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS includes 'blocked' alongside 'done'/'failed'; (2) advance.py's on_blocked=halt branch calls ensure_phase_label with 'blocked' as the terminal key; (3) test_phase_labels.py test_ensure_phase_label_terminal_key exercises the 'done' terminal path, confirming the pattern; (4) 'blocked' is the conclusion written when a gate's on_blocked fires — confirmed terminal in the engine's lifecycle

blast_radius.why: Definition matches existing usage across PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS and advance.py; no semantic change is introduced

reversibility.why: If the set of terminal states changes, the function body is a one-line edit to the set literal

ledger.revisit_if: The engine adds a fourth terminal state or renames 'blocked'
